html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

“Be warned, assisting the Cryptic Founder will alter your world and cannot be undone. Much will be lost and gained in the name of Progress. Are you sure you'd like to continue?”

–The Price of Ambition

Contents

  • 1 Activating
  • 2 Lunar Rift
  • 3 Lunar Thralls
    • 3.1 Deadly Brightshade
  • 4 Relevant Content
    • 4.1 Items
    • 4.2 Structures
    • 4.3 Mobs
    • 4.4 Mechanics
    • 4.5 Pre Rift Content
  • 5 Tips
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Gallery

Activating

Warning message prior to activating the Lunar Rifts.

After players defeat the Celestial Champion, the Grainy Transmission will appear near a Lunar Essence Extractor and ask for an Enlightened Shard. Players can show and then give the Enlightened Shard to the Grainy Transmission. If the players choose not to give the Shard, the Grainy Transmission will randomly appear somewhere on the surface of the world, and players have another chance to give him the shard.

Once assisting the Grainy Transmission, the world will change irreversibly:

  • The Lunar Rift spawns periodically.
  • Abandoned Junk spawns and the Grainy Transmission hints at their location periodically.
  • Scrappy Werepig drops different loot.
  • During Full Moon night, Player's Sanity is replaced by Enlightenment.

Lunar Rift

After assisting Wagstaff, the Lunar Rifts begin to spawn in Forest world periodically.

Lunar Rifts can appear on any land tile only within the mainland (thus excluding any islands out at sea), as long as there isn't a Structure, Wall or transplanted Plant in a 5-Tile radius (this excludes player-planted trees).

Once it spawns, the Turf around the Rift slowly gets covered by Lunar Rift Floor, and is inhabited by Grazers. Ryftstals also frequently appear around the Rift. When a character stands on the Lunar Rift Floor, their Sanity is replaced with Enlightenment, similar to the surface of the Lunar Island and the inside of Moonstorms.

The Rift has three stages in total. Every 4-5 days after its initial appearance it advances to the next stage. The transition is accompanied by an explosion that deals 60 damage to all creatures in the area and destroys Structures. The stage determines the distance in which the Turf will spread, as well as the number of Grazers and Ryftstals that appear:

Phase Radius
1 1 1 0
2 3 3 4
3 6 6 12

Every 0.75-1.25 in-game days, the Rift attempts to create Ryftstals, unless the maximum amount of Ryftstals have been reached for the current stage.

While a Lunar Rift exist, it has several additional effects on the forest world.

  • Lunar Hail happens periodically.
  • Certain creatures are mutated by Gestalts after their death, becoming these versions:
    • Crystal Deerclops
    • Armored Bearger
    • Possessed Varg
  • Bright-Eyed Frogs have a 60% chance to replace a regular Frog during Frog Rain.

Lunar Thralls

Each time the Rift spawns, a Lunar Thrall is randomly selected to be released into the world, and each Lunar Thrall is released in a different way.

Here is a full list of each Lunar Thralls:

Deadly Brightshade

When the Rift reaches its final stage, it picks a number between 4 and 6 to decide how many Deadly Brightshades waves will appear before it closes. Afterwards, it immediately sends the first wave of Incursive Gestalt to attack groups of plants it thinks are "gardens." The other waves come every 2 days. After the last wave, the Rift closes. It then picks a new spot and reopens 5 days later.

Relevant Content

Items

  • Brightshade Helm
  • Brightshade Armor
  • Brightshade Bomb
  • Brightshade Staff
  • Brightshade Sword
  • Brightshade Smasher
  • Brightshade Shoevel
  • Brightshade Repair Kit
  • Polar Bearger Bin
  • Ice Crystaleyezer Kit
  • Howlitzer
  • Brightshade Husk
  • Empty Spark Ark
  • Spark Ark
  • Research Notes
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor
  • Elastispacer
  • Hermit Rehomer
  • Wind Charm
  • Bloomers
  • Stockings
  • Petal Tea
  • Dark Petal Tea
  • Foliage Tea
  • Succulent Tea
  • Lune Tree Blossom Tea
  • Fire Nettle Tea
  • Tillweed Tea
  • Forget-Me-Lots Tea
  • Deep Freeze Staff
  • Grit Grinder
  • Desiccant Sack
  • Portage Conch
  • Substrate Extrapolator
  • Phasmo-Encapsulator
  • Gyroscopic Transduction Core
  • Celestial Jewel
  • Glove
  • Clipboard
  • Nucleation Fluid
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Base Kit
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Parts
  • Static Restrainer
  • Restrained Static

Structures

  • Lunar Rift
  • Ryftstal
  • Abandoned Junk
  • Deerclops Carcass
  • Bearger Carcass
  • Varg Carcass
  • Ice Crystaleyezer
  • Pearl's Light Post
  • Pearl's Tea Shop
  • Pearl's Hot Spring
  • Large Drying Rack
  • Notional Fabricator
  • Kinetoclasmic Field Expandinator
  • Fulcronial Selector
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Base
  • Unrestrained Static

Mobs

  • Grazer
  • Bright-Eyed Frog
  • Incursive Gestalt
  • Deadly Brightshade
  • Crystal Deerclops
  • Armored Bearger
  • Possessed Varg
  • Terramite
  • Warbler
  • Celestial Revenant
  • Enlightenment W.A.R.B.O.T.
  • Celestial Scion
  • Iminical Gestalt
  • Bright-Beaked Bird
  • Crystal-Crested Buzzard

Mechanics

  • Spark Ark Quest
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Quest
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Quest
  • Lunar Hail


Pre Rift Content

All content listed below is closely related to Lunar Rift content, but can be obtained before it opens.

  • Enlightened Shard
  • Scrap
  • Auto-Mat-O-Chanic
  • Brightsmithy Kit
  • Brightsmithy
  • Pure Brilliance
  • Bright Boxer
  • Combriner
  • Flash Freeze Staff
  • Grit Duster
  • Desiccant Pouch
  • Brightsmithy Filter
  • Planar Damage
  • Planar Defense
  • Planar Entity Protection

Tips

  • The Lunar Rift can be enabled from day 1 in the World Settings.
  • Lunar Rift can be spawned with console command c_spawnrift()

Trivia

  • Lunar Rift was introduced in the Taking Root update.

Gallery

  • On Lunar Quest Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

The Lunar Rift spawns periodically.

Source

Miscellaneous
"lunarrift_portal"
“All that science hiding inside... and I can't get to it!”
–Wilson, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Uh... is that supposed to be here?”
–Willow, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Let Wolfgang in!”
–Wolfgang, when examining the Lunar Rift
“A gaping wound in the sky.”
–Wendy, when examining the Lunar Rift
“ACCESS DENIED”
–WX-78, when examining the Lunar Rift
“It seems to be contaminating the surrounding area.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the Lunar Rift
“It's givin' me goosebumps... I hate goosebumps.”
–Woodie, when examining the Lunar Rift
“I don't like the look of this.”
–Maxwell, when examining the Lunar Rift
“An opening, yet I cannot pass through.”
–Wigfrid, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Aww, it won't let us in.”
–Webber, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Ah, zut! It will not let me through.”
–Warly, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Hello?”
–Wormwood, when examining the Lunar Rift
“That tear's a bit beyond what my trusty tape can fix.”
–Winona, when examining the Lunar Rift
“It's all falling apart at the seams!”
–Wortox, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Who broke sky?!”
–Wurt, when examining the Lunar Rift
“It looks like a rip in the sky. Neat!”
–Walter, when examining the Lunar Rift
“Sloppy! Just look at those rough edges!”
–Wanda, when examining the Lunar Rift

Lunar Rifts can appear on any land tile only within the mainland (thus excluding any islands out at sea), as long as there isn't a [[Structures|Structure]], [[Wall]] or transplanted [[Plant]] in a 5-Tile radius (this excludes player-planted trees). Once it spawns, the [[Turfs|Turf]] around the Rift slowly gets covered by [[Lunar Rift Floor]], and is inhabited by [[Grazer]]s. [[Ryftstal]]s also frequently appear around the Rift. When a character stands on the [[Lunar Rift Floor]], their [[Sanity]] is replaced with [[Enlightenment]], similar to the surface of the [[Lunar Island]] and the inside of [[Moonstorm]]s. The Rift has three stages in total. Every 4-5 days after its initial appearance it advances to the next stage. The transition is accompanied by an explosion that deals 60 damage to all creatures in the area and destroys [[Structures]]. While a Lunar Rift exist, it has several additional effects on the forest world. * [[Lunar Hail]] happens periodically. * Certain creatures are mutated by Gestalts after their death, becoming these versions: **[[Crystal Deerclops]] **[[Armored Bearger]] **[[Possessed Varg]] * [[Bright-Eyed Frog]]s have a 60% chance to replace a regular Frog during [[Frog Rain]]. ==Lunar Thralls== Each time the Rift spawns, a Lunar Thrall is randomly selected to be released into the world, and each Lunar Thrall is released in a different way. Here is a full list of each Lunar Thralls: ===Deadly Brightshade=== When the Rift reaches its final stage, it picks a number between 4 and 6 to decide how many [[Deadly Brightshade]]s waves will appear before it closes. Afterwards, it immediately sends the first wave of [[Incursive Gestalt]] to attack groups of plants it thinks are "gardens." The other waves come every 2 days. After the last wave, the Rift closes. File:Enlightened Shard.png|[[Enlightened Shard]] File:Scrap.png|[[Scrap]] File:Auto-Mat-O-Chanic.png|[[Auto-Mat-O-Chanic]] File:Brightsmithy Kit.png|[[Brightsmithy Kit]] File:Brightsmithy.png|[[Brightsmithy]] File:Pure Brilliance.png|[[Pure Brilliance]] File:Bright Boxer.png|[[Bright Boxer]] File:Combriner.png|[[Combriner]] File:Flash Freeze Staff.png|[[Flash Freeze Staff]] File:Grit Duster.png|[[Grit Duster]] File:Desiccant Pouch.png|[[Desiccant Pouch]] * [[Brightsmithy Filter]] * [[Planar Damage]] * [[Planar Defense]] * [[Planar Entity Protection]] == {{Tips}} Tips == * The Lunar Rift can be enabled from day 1 in the [[World Customization/Don't Starve Together#World Settings for Caves|World Settings]]. * Lunar Rift can be spawned with console command c_spawnrift() == {{Trivia}} Trivia == * Lunar Rift was introduced in the ''[[Taking Root]]'' update. == [[File:Blueprint.png|32px]] Gallery == RWP HT Lunar Quest Concept Art.png|On Lunar Quest Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from ''Rhymes with Play''. {{Structures}} [[Category:From Beyond]] [[zh:月亮裂隙]] [[vi:Lunar_Rift]]
